title: Connect your dev team to business goals
excerpt: Join us live to see how monday dev’s new Hybrid Portfolio connects engineering, product, GTM, and leadership around the same goals, workflows, and metrics.

posts.author: Omer (Omer Fast)
posts.cookedHtml: Join us live to see how monday dev’s new Hybrid Portfolio connects engineering, product, GTM, and leadership around the same goals, workflows, and metrics.

posts.author: jameswood32 (James Wood )
posts.cookedHtml:
Bridging the gap between development teams and business objectives is crucial for delivering impactful results. Here are some practical ways to make that connection stronger:

  1. Transparent Communication:
    Regularly share business goals, key metrics, and company vision with the dev team. This helps them understand why their work matters beyond just writing code.

  2. Involve Devs Early:
    Bring developers into planning sessions and discussions about product direction. When they see the bigger picture, they can align their work to solve real business problems.

  3. Set Clear, Outcome-Oriented Goals:
    Translate business goals into technical objectives or user stories. For example, if the business goal is “increase user retention by 10%,” frame development tasks around features or improvements that contribute to that.

  4. Use OKRs or KPIs:
    Implement Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) or Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) that link development efforts to measurable business outcomes.

  5. Foster Cross-Functional Collaboration:
    Encourage developers to work closely with product managers, marketers, and sales teams. This promotes understanding and shared ownership of goals.

  6. Celebrate Business Impact:
    When a feature or fix contributes to a business win, celebrate it! Recognizing the impact boosts motivation and reinforces the connection.

  7. Provide Context, Not Just Tasks:
    Instead of assigning isolated tasks, explain how the task fits into the overall business strategy and customer value.

In short: Help your dev team see beyond the code by clearly connecting their work to real business impact. When developers understand the “why,” they’ll be more engaged, innovative, and aligned with company success.
